name: Desktop / rich-client application
summary: An installed graphical application running on a user's own machine (Windows/macOS/Linux), often operating offline or occasionally-connected with local data and background sync. Its distinctive concerns are install/update, OS integration and permissions, local persistence and resource use rather than server-side scaling.

concerns: cross-platform packaging and installers (MSI/MSIX, .pkg/.dmg, deb/rpm/AppImage/Flatpak/Snap)
code signing and notarization (Authenticode, Apple notarization/Gatekeeper) and the trust chain
secure auto-update: signature verification, delta updates, staged rollout and downgrade protection
per-OS UI toolkit and rendering differences
supported OS versions and architectures (x64/arm64)
questions: Are per-OS packaging, code signing and notarization specified?
Is the auto-update mechanism signed, staged and protected against downgrade/MITM?
Are supported OS versions and architectures stated?

concerns: offline / occasionally-connected operation with clear online/offline/queued status indication
local configuration and profile management
enterprise-managed deployment (MDM/GPO/Intune) and update channels (stable/beta)
offline licensing/activation
remote config and diagnostics collection
questions: Is offline / intermittently-connected operation (with queued-state indicators) specified?
Are enterprise deployment/management (MDM/GPO) and update channels defined?
Is offline activation/licensing addressed?

concerns: local persistent storage (embedded SQLite/DB, files) with integrity and at-rest encryption
sync to server with conflict detection and resolution (CRDT / last-writer-wins / merge)
offline edit queue and eventual reconciliation
local schema/data migration across app versions
cache invalidation and secure deletion of local data
questions: Is the local-storage model, its encryption and integrity specified?
Is the sync conflict-detection/resolution policy defined for offline edits?
Is local-data migration across versions handled?

concerns: OS permission model and least privilege (filesystem, camera/mic, keychain/credential store)
protection of local secrets/tokens via OS keystore (Windows DPAPI/Credential Manager, macOS Keychain, libsecret)
renderer/plugin sandboxing and IPC hardening (e.g. Electron contextIsolation, disabled nodeIntegration)
protection against local privilege escalation and DLL/dylib hijacking / binary tampering
update and code-signing integrity verification at launch
questions: Are requested OS permissions minimised and secrets stored in the OS keystore?
Is the renderer/plugin sandbox and IPC hardened against untrusted content?
Is protection against binary hijacking/tampering specified?

concerns: native per-OS look-and-feel and conventions (menus, keyboard shortcuts, window management, dark mode)
UI-thread responsiveness (no freezes during long operations; background work off the main thread)
consistent cross-platform behaviour vs native expectations
user-error protection on destructive local actions
first-run onboarding and discoverability
questions: Are per-OS UI conventions (menus, shortcuts, dark mode) and theming specified?
Is UI responsiveness under long operations required (no freezes)?
Are safeguards for destructive local actions defined?

concerns: exposure of custom controls to the platform accessibility API (UI Automation on Windows, NSAccessibility on macOS, AT-SPI on Linux)
full keyboard-only operation and focus management
screen-reader support (Narrator/VoiceOver/Orca)
WCAG 2.2 / EN 301 549 conformance where mandated (esp. public-sector procurement)
respect for OS scaling/DPI, high-contrast and reduced-motion settings
questions: Do custom controls expose the platform accessibility API (UIA/NSAccessibility/AT-SPI)?
Are full keyboard operation and screen-reader support required?
Are OS scaling, high-contrast and reduced-motion respected?

concerns: crash reporting and symbolication (minidumps/Crashpad/Sentry) with opt-in consent
auto-recovery of unsaved work (autosave/journaling)
clean rollback on failed update
resilience to connectivity loss mid-operation without data loss
watchdog for hung UI
typical_requirements: crash reporting (opt-in) & recovery of unsaved work
questions: Is crash reporting (with consent) and recovery of unsaved work specified?
Does a failed update roll back cleanly?
Is mid-operation connectivity loss handled without data loss?

concerns: resource footprint: memory, idle CPU, battery/energy impact and disk usage
startup / cold-launch time
responsiveness on modest target hardware
background-activity limits when idle or on battery
questions: Are memory/CPU/battery and startup budgets specified for the target hardware?
Are background/idle resource limits defined?

concerns: privacy-scoped diagnostic logging and telemetry for adoption/error rates
feature flags and staged rollout of components
modular in-place update of components
reproducible and signed builds
questions: Are diagnostic logging and telemetry (privacy-scoped) and feature flags specified?
Are builds reproducible and signed?

concerns: telemetry/analytics consent (opt-in vs opt-out) and GDPR obligations for a fat client
local storage of PII and its protection
crash-dump content scrubbing (dumps may contain PII/secrets)
bundled third-party/OSS license notices
questions: Is telemetry consent (opt-in/opt-out) and GDPR handling specified?
Are crash dumps scrubbed of PII/secrets?
Are bundled third-party license notices included?
